summary: Multiple ballistic and cruise missile launches targeted Bila Tserkva and surrounding areas, while Russian UAV groups intensified along the Kharkiv‑Sumy border. Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the south‑east front, and a Russian drone struck a residential building in Galaţi, Romania. Ukraine announced increased defense spending and sought to assist Romania with air‑defence support.
key_points: Several ballistic missiles were reported launched toward Bila Tserkva from unknown launch sites (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67274).
A Shahed missile struck Bila Tserkva, prompting alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67266).
Cruise missiles from Skvyra were observed heading to Bila Tserkva (Source: @eRadarrua/94219).
Russian tactical aviation activity increased on the south‑east axis, threatening front‑line regions (Source: @kpszsu/63223).
UAV groups were detected along the Kharkiv‑Sumy border, including new groups near Shevchenkove and Pavlohrad (Source: @eRadarrua/94182).
A Russian drone entered Romanian airspace and hit a multi‑storey building in Galaţi, causing a fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81528).
Ukraine offered air‑defence assistance to Romania to counter Russian drone threats (Source: @UkraineNow/81540).

sections.executive_summary: Multiple ballistic and cruise missiles struck or were launched toward Bila Tserkva, indicating heightened Russian strike activity. Russian UAV formations increased along the Kharkiv‑Sumy border and tactical aviation activity rose on the south‑east front. A Russian drone attack in Galaţi, Romania, prompted Ukraine to propose air‑defence cooperation with Bucharest.

points: Ukraine’s defense budget was increased to 1.56 trillion hryvnias and a €90 billion EU loan was ratified, reflecting a major financing boost for military procurement (Source: @UkraineNow/81523).
President Zelensky announced that Russia is beginning the first stage of a massed attack, mentioning “winged missiles” and isolated launches (Source: @UkraineNow/81539).
Ukraine pledged to help Romania strengthen its sky defence against Russian drones, with Ukrainian specialists to work on the project (Source: @UkraineNow/81540).
Ukrainian officials warned that small Belarusian units are present on the border, but a larger invasion from Belarus cannot be ruled out (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30518).
Ballistic missiles were launched from the Bryansk region toward Ukrainian territory (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67277).
Russian tactical aviation was reported active on the south‑east direction, posing a threat to front‑line oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/63223).
Russian forces employed cruise missiles possibly launched from a Tu‑95MS bomber to deplete air‑defence missile stocks (Source: @eRadarrua/94206).
UAVs originating from Belgorod oblast were observed moving south into Sumy region (Source: @kpszsu/63220).
UAV groups were reported near the Kharkiv‑Sumy border, including two new groups north of Shevchenkove and one near Pavlohrad (Source: @eRadarrua/94182).
Three UAV groups on Kharkiv front were heading toward Valki from the north (Source: @eRadarrua/94231).
UAVs from Belgorod were moving south into Sumy, increasing pressure on eastern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/63220).
Controlled UAVs were sighted moving from Dnipropetrovsk toward Pavlohrad and from Donetsk toward Sloviansk (Source: @eRadarrua/94230).
UAV activity was noted in Chernihiv heading toward Ichnia from the north (Source: @kpszsu/63225).
UAVs were observed over Chernihiv moving toward Ripky (Source: @eRadarrua/94230).
UAVs from Belgorod were reported heading toward the north‑west of Sumy and Poltava directions (Source: @kpszsu/63221).
A UAV group was detected near Chernihiv moving toward Ichnia (Source: @kpszsu/63225).
Cruise missiles were launched from Skvyra toward Bila Tserkva and from Skvyra toward Pogrebische (Vinnytsia region) (Source: @eRadarrua/94219).
A Shahed missile struck Bila Tserkva, prompting local alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67266).
Rocket launches were reported from southern Kyiv oblast toward Vinnytsia and from southern Kyiv toward Kirovohrad (Source: @kpszsu/63250).
UAVs were observed in Cherkasy heading toward Pereyaslav (Source: @kpszsu/63233).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the south‑east axis, with threats of air‑to‑ground strikes on front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/63223).
Controlled aerial bombs were launched by Russian tactical aviation onto Sumy region (Source: @kpszsu/63254).
Air‑raid alerts were issued over Kyiv at 19:08 on 29 May 2026 (Source: @eRadarrua/94185).
A Russian drone entered Romanian airspace and impacted a residential building in Galaţi, causing a fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81528).

key_developments: Ballistic missiles were launched toward Bila Tserkva, indicating a concentrated strike effort on that locality (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67274).
A Shahed missile impacted Bila Tserkva, confirming the use of loitering munitions in the area (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67266).
Cruise missiles from Skvyra were observed heading to Bila Tserkva and Pogrebische, expanding the missile threat envelope (Source: @eRadarrua/94219).
Russian tactical aviation activity increased on the south‑east front, threatening front‑line oblasts with air‑to‑ground attacks (Source: @kpszsu/63223).
UAV groups intensified along the Kharkiv‑Sumy border, with new formations near Shevchenkove and Pavlohrad (Source: @eRadarrua/94182).
A Russian drone struck a multi‑storey building in Galaţi, Romania, causing a fire and civilian casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81528).
Ukraine pledged air‑defence assistance to Romania to counter Russian drone incursions (Source: @UkraineNow/81540).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian missile and UAV activity is concentrating on Bila Tserkva and the eastern border regions, suggesting a strategy to disrupt Ukrainian logistics and command nodes. The deployment of tactical aviation and controlled aerial bombs on the south‑east front raises the risk of rapid escalation and higher civilian exposure. Cross‑border drone attacks into Romania demonstrate an expanding operational reach, potentially prompting broader NATO involvement.
sections.regional_response: NATO members, including the United States and Finland, have publicly condemned recent Russian strikes and pledged support for Romanian air‑defence, indicating a possible increase in collective security measures. Ukraine's request for assistance and its own budget expansion aim to bolster defensive capabilities across multiple fronts. Regional governments are likely to heighten alert levels and coordinate air‑defence integration with Ukrainian forces.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of missile launch sites, especially in Bryansk and Skvyra, should be intensified to anticipate further strikes on Bila Tserkva. UAV traffic along the Kharkiv‑Sumy corridor suggests preparation for reconnaissance or strike missions; SIGINT assets should focus on command‑and‑control nodes. The emergence of cross‑border drone threats necessitates sharing of radar and interception data with Romanian authorities.
